title: Real-time inference for binary neutron star mergers using machine learning
data_sources: LVK O2/O3 and design-sensitivity noise PSDs

data_scope.injection_study_1: 200 GW170817-like simulations at varying noise levels
data_scope.injection_study_2: 10000 simulated BNS datasets with random priors

core_conclusion: DINGO-BNS performs complete Bayesian parameter estimation of all 17 binary neutron star parameters in just 1 second using simulation-based inference with normalizing flows, three orders of magnitude faster than conventional methods and without sacrificing accuracy. Novel prior conditioning enables event-specific prior tuning at inference time without retraining; physics-informed data compression (heterodyning + multibanding) achieves 60-650x lossless dimensionality reduction. Pre-merger inference delivers ~30% improved sky localization over BAYESTAR and scales to hour-long signals for next-generation detectors.
why_it_matters: Enables true real-time multi-messenger astronomy (1 s vs. 2 h for full Bayesian inference); first framework for pre-merger parameter estimation enabling telescope slewing before coalescence; no accuracy trade-off, validated by importance sampling on real LVK data; provides a blueprint for next-generation GW detectors (Cosmic Explorer, Einstein Telescope); fully open-source with trained network on Zenodo.
why_nature: Three-orders-of-magnitude speedup on a flagship astronomical problem; cross-disciplinary impact spanning cosmology, nuclear physics, gravity, and ML; validated on real landmark data (GW170817, GW190425); decade-long implications as blueprint for future GW detector data analysis.
added_value_to_my_research: Prior conditioning as adaptive bandwidth selection: train a flow-KDE with a hyperprior over bandwidths, condition on local density at inference time for amortized R2SFCA catchment radii
Heterodyning analogy for spatiotemporal data: factor out known periodic/trend structure before GNN encoding, reducing effective dimensionality of mobile-phone and GPS trajectory data

Amortized population inference for multilevel spatial models: produce instant per-unit posteriors (e.g., per-neighborhood access distributions) and aggregate hierarchically into regional MGWR estimates
Importance-sampling efficiency as geospatial distributional shift detector: per-location reliability scores flagging where neural spatial models should not be trusted

Continuous-stream inference architecture as blueprint for real-time health surveillance: amortized flow trained on epidemic-mobility simulations, sliding-window trigger, 1-s-latency spatiotemporal risk maps

related.title: Probabilistic weather forecasting with machine learning
related.relation: amortized probabilistic prediction from simulation-trained neural networks, with importance-sampling-style calibration

related.title: Promising directions of machine learning for partial differential equations
related.relation: physics-informed data compression and operator learning as alternative to DINGO-BNS heterodyning strategy

related.title: Machine learning at the energy and intensity frontiers of particle physics
related.relation: ML for real-time inference in high-energy physics, analogous amortized inference challenges and solutions

related.title: Deep transfer operator learning for partial differential equations under conditional shift
related.relation: transfer of trained scientific ML models to out-of-distribution conditions, parallel to DINGO-BNS generalization to next-generation detectors

related.title: Mobility-driven synthetic contact matrices as a scalable solution for real-time pandemic response modeling
related.relation: real-time continuous-stream inference pipeline applied to mobility data, direct analogy to DINGO-BNS sliding-window scanner

figures.caption: Real-time inference of GW170817: DINGO-BNS estimates all BNS parameters in 1 s (orange, 10.8% efficiency), reproducing LVK results three orders of magnitude faster, with pre-merger capability (teal, 78.9% efficiency)
Pre-merger inference: evolution of 90% credible sky area, chirp mass uncertainty, SNR, and Bayes factor; 30% localization improvement over BAYESTAR; Cosmic Explorer pre-merger sky map
Prior conditioning and method flowchart: event-specific chirp mass prior selection, heterodyning, multibanding, and end-to-end neural network architecture
Compression and frequency masking: heterodyning simplifies signal phase evolution; multibanding achieves adaptive 60-650x compression; frequency masking enables pre-merger analysis
